NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “Do Not Destroy.” A Psalm of Asaph. A Song. We give thanks to You, O God, we give thanks! For Your wondrous works declare that Your name is near.
NKJV.2: “When I choose the proper time, I will judge uprightly.
NKJV.3: The earth and all its inhabitants are dissolved; I set up its pillars firmly. Selah
NKJV.4: “I said to the boastful, ‘Do not deal boastfully,’ And to the wicked, ‘Do not lift up the horn.
NKJV.5: Do not lift up your horn on high; Do not speak with a stiff neck.’ ”
NKJV.6: For exaltation comes neither from the east Nor from the west nor from the south.
NKJV.7: But God is the Judge: He puts down one, And exalts another.
NKJV.8: For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup, And the wine is red; It is fully mixed, and He pours it out; Surely its dregs shall all the wicked of the earth Drain and drink down.
NKJV.9: But I will declare forever, I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
NKJV.10: “All the horns of the wicked I will also cut off, But the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.”

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, Altaschith<S>516</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> or Song<S>7892</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. Unto thee, O God<S>430</S>, do we give thanks<S>3034</S>, unto thee do we give thanks<S>3034</S>: for that thy name<S>8034</S> is near<S>7138</S> thy wondrous works<S>6381</S> declare<S>5608</S>. <sup>Altaschith: or, Destroy not</sup> <sup>of: or, for</sup>
KJV.2: When I shall receive<S>3947</S> the congregation<S>4150</S> I will judge<S>8199</S> uprightly<S>4339</S>. <sup>receive: or, take a set time</sup>
KJV.3: The earth<S>776</S> and all the inhabitants<S>3427</S> thereof are dissolved<S>4127</S>: I bear up<S>8505</S> the pillars<S>5982</S> of it. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.4: I said<S>559</S> unto the fools<S>1984</S>, Deal not foolishly<S>1984</S>: and to the wicked<S>7563</S>, Lift not up<S>7311</S> the horn<S>7161</S>:
KJV.5: Lift not up<S>7311</S> your horn<S>7161</S> on high<S>4791</S>: speak<S>1696</S> not with a stiff<S>6277</S> neck<S>6677</S>.
KJV.6: For promotion<S>7311</S> <S>2022</S> cometh neither from the east<S>4161</S>, nor from the west<S>4628</S>, nor from the south<S>4057</S>. <sup>south: Heb. desert</sup>
KJV.7: But God<S>430</S> is the judge<S>8199</S>: he putteth down<S>8213</S> one, and setteth up<S>7311</S> another.
KJV.8: For in the hand<S>3027</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> there is a cup<S>3563</S>, and the wine<S>3196</S> is red<S>2560</S>; it is full<S>4392</S> of mixture<S>4538</S>; and he poureth out<S>5064</S> of the same<S>2088</S>: but the dregs<S>8105</S> thereof, all the wicked<S>7563</S> of the earth<S>776</S> shall wring them out<S>4680</S>, and drink<S>8354</S> them.
KJV.9: But I will declare<S>5046</S> for ever<S>5769</S>; I will sing praises<S>2167</S> to the God<S>430</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>.
KJV.10: All the horns<S>7161</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S> also will I cut off<S>1438</S>; but the horns<S>7161</S> of the righteous<S>6662</S> shall be exalted<S>7311</S>.

ESV.1: We give thanks to you, O God; we give thanks, for your name is near. We  recount your wondrous deeds.
ESV.2: “At the set time that I appoint I will judge with equity.
ESV.3: When the earth totters, and all its inhabitants, it is I who keep steady its pillars. Selah
ESV.4: I say to the boastful, ‘Do not boast,’ and to the wicked, ‘Do not lift up your horn;
ESV.5: do not lift up your horn on high, or speak with haughty neck.’”
ESV.6: For not from the east or from the west and not from the wilderness comes lifting up,
ESV.7: but it is God who executes judgment, putting down one and lifting up another.
ESV.8: For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup with foaming wine, well mixed, and he pours out from it, and all the wicked of the earth shall drain it down to the dregs.
ESV.9: But I will declare it forever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
ESV.10: All the horns of the wicked I will cut off, but the horns of the righteous shall be lifted up.

NASB.1: For the choir director; [set to] Al-tashheth. A Psalm of Asaph, a Song. We give thanks to You, O God, we give thanks, For Your name is near; Men declare Your wondrous works.
NASB.2: 'When I select an appointed time, It is I who judge with equity.
NASB.3: 'The earth and all who dwell in it melt; It is I who have firmly set its pillars. Selah.
NASB.4: 'I said to the boastful, 'Do not boast,' And to the wicked, 'Do not lift up the horn;
NASB.5: Do not lift up your horn on high, Do not speak with insolent pride.''
NASB.6: For not from the east, nor from the west, Nor from the desert [comes] exaltation;
NASB.7: But God is the Judge; He puts down one and exalts another.
NASB.8: For a cup is in the hand of the LORD, and the wine foams; It is well mixed, and He pours out of this; Surely all the wicked of the earth must drain [and] drink down its dregs.
NASB.9: But as for me, I will declare [it] forever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
NASB.10: And all the horns of the wicked He will cut off, [But] the horns of the righteous will be lifted up.

NLT.1:
We thank you, O God!
We give thanks because you are near.
People everywhere tell of your wonderful deeds.
NLT.2:
God says, “At the time I have planned,
I will bring justice against the wicked.
NLT.3:
When the earth quakes and its people live in turmoil,
I am the one who keeps its foundations firm. Interlude
NLT.4:
“I warned the proud, ‘Stop your boasting!’
I told the wicked, ‘Don’t raise your fists!
NLT.5:
Don’t raise your fists in defiance at the heavens
or speak with such arrogance.’”
NLT.6:
For no one on earth — from east or west,
or even from the wilderness —
should raise a defiant fist.
NLT.7:
It is God alone who judges;
he decides who will rise and who will fall.
NLT.8:
For the LORD holds a cup in his hand
that is full of foaming wine mixed with spices.
He pours out the wine in judgment,
and all the wicked must drink it,
draining it to the dregs.
NLT.9:
But as for me, I will always proclaim what God has done;
I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
NLT.10:
For God says, “I will break the strength of the wicked,
but I will increase the power of the godly.”

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A Psalm by Asaph. A song. We give thanks to you, God. We give thanks, for your Name is near. Men tell about your wondrous works.
WEB.2: When I choose the appointed time, I will judge blamelessly.
WEB.3: The earth and all its inhabitants quake. I firmly hold its pillars. Selah.
WEB.4: I said to the arrogant, “Don’t boast!” I said to the wicked, “Don’t lift up the horn.
WEB.5: Don’t lift up your horn on high. Don’t speak with a stiff neck.”
WEB.6: For neither from the east, nor from the west, nor yet from the south, comes exaltation.
WEB.7: But God is the judge. He puts down one, and lifts up another.
WEB.8: For in Yahweh’s hand there is a cup, full of foaming wine mixed with spices. He pours it out. Indeed the wicked of the earth drink and drink it to its very dregs.
WEB.9: But I will declare this forever: I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
WEB.10: I will cut off all the horns of the wicked, but the horns of the righteous shall be lifted up.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: “Do Not Destroy.” A psalm of Asaph. ⓝ A song. [1] We give thanks to you, God;
we give thanks to you, for your name is near.
People tell about your wondrous works. ⓐ
CSB17.2:
“When I choose a time,
I will judge fairly. ⓑ
CSB17.3:
When the earth and all its inhabitants shake,
I am the one who steadies its pillars. ⓒ Selah
CSB17.4:
I say to the boastful, ‘Do not boast,’
and to the wicked, ‘Do not lift up your horn. ⓓ
CSB17.5:
Do not lift up your horn against heaven [1]
or speak arrogantly.’” ⓔ
CSB17.6:
Exaltation does not come
from the east, the west, or the desert,
CSB17.7:
for God is the Judge:
He brings down one and exalts another. ⓕ
CSB17.8:
For there is a cup in the LORD’s hand,
full of wine blended with spices, and he pours from it.
All the wicked of the earth will drink,
draining it to the dregs. ⓖ
CSB17.9:
As for me, I will tell about him forever;
I will sing praise to the God of Jacob. ⓗ
CSB17.10:
“I will cut off all the horns of the wicked, ⓘ
but the horns of the righteous will be lifted up.” ⓙ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to [the tune of] "Do Not Destroy." A Psalm of Asaph. A Song. We give thanks and praise to You, O God, we give thanks,
For Your [wonderful works declare that Your] name is near;
People declare Your wonders.
AMP.2:
"When I select an appointed time,
I will judge with equity," [says the LORD].
AMP.3:
"The earth and all the inhabitants of it melt [in tumultuous times].
It is I who will steady its pillars. Selah.
AMP.4:
"I said to the arrogant, 'Do not boast;'
And to the wicked, 'Do not lift up the horn [of self-glorification].
AMP.5:
'Do not lift up your [defiant and aggressive] horn on high,
Do not speak with a stiff neck.'"
AMP.6:
For not from the east, nor from the west,
Nor from the desert comes exaltation.
AMP.7:
But God is the Judge;
He puts down one and lifts up another.
AMP.8:
For a cup [of His wrath] is in the hand of the LORD, and the wine foams;
It is well mixed and fully spiced, and He pours out from it;
And all the wicked of the earth must drain it and drink down to its dregs.
AMP.9:
But as for me, I will declare it and rejoice forever;
I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
AMP.10:
All the horns of the wicked He will cut off,
But the horns of the righteous will be lifted up.

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
We thank you, God, we thank you—
your Name is our favorite word;
your mighty works are all we talk about.
MSG.2:
You say, "I'm calling this meeting to order,
I'm ready to set things right.
MSG.3:
When the earth goes topsy-turvy
And nobody knows which end is up,
I nail it all down,
I put everything in place again.
MSG.4:
I say to the smart alecks, 'That's enough,'
to the bullies, 'Not so fast.' "
MSG.5:
Don't raise your fist against High God.
Don't raise your voice against Rock of Ages.
MSG.6:
He's the One from east to west;
from desert to mountains, he's the One.
MSG.7:
God rules: he brings this one down to his knees,
pulls that one up on her feet.
MSG.8:
God has a cup in his hand,
a bowl of wine, full to the brim.
He draws from it and pours;
it's drained to the dregs.
Earth's wicked ones drink it all,
drink it down to the last bitter drop!
MSG.9:
And I'm telling the story of God Eternal,
singing the praises of Jacob's God.
MSG.10:
The fists of the wicked
are bloody stumps,
The arms of the righteous
are lofty green branches.

NIV.1:
Psalm 75
For the director of music. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A psalm of Asaph. A song.
We give thanks to you, O God,
we give thanks, for your Name is near;
men tell of your wonderful deeds.
NIV.2:
You say, “I choose the appointed time;
it is I who judge uprightly.
NIV.3:
When the earth and all its people quake,
it is I who hold its pillars firm. Selah
NIV.4:
To the arrogant I say, ‘Boast no more,’
and to the wicked, ‘Do not lift up your horns.
NIV.5:
Do not lift your horns against heaven;
do not speak with outstretched neck.’”
NIV.6:
No one from the east or the west
or from the desert can exalt a man.
NIV.7:
But it is God who judges:
He brings one down, he exalts another.
NIV.8:
In the hand of the Lord is a cup
full of foaming wine mixed with spices;
he pours it out, and all the wicked of the earth
drink it down to its very dregs.
NIV.9:
As for me, I will declare this forever;
I will sing praise to the God of Jacob.
NIV.10:
I will cut off the horns of all the wicked,
but the horns of the righteous will be lifted up.

NRSVCE.1:
We give thanks to you, O God;
we give thanks; your name is near.
People tell of your wondrous deeds.
NRSVCE.2:
At the set time that I appoint
I will judge with equity.
NRSVCE.3:
When the earth totters, with all its inhabitants,
it is I who keep its pillars steady. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
I say to the boastful, “Do not boast,”
and to the wicked, “Do not lift up your horn;
NRSVCE.5:
do not lift up your horn on high,
or speak with insolent neck.”
NRSVCE.6:
For not from the east or from the west
and not from the wilderness comes lifting up;
NRSVCE.7:
but it is God who executes judgment,
putting down one and lifting up another.
NRSVCE.8:
For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup
with foaming wine, well mixed;
he will pour a draught from it,
and all the wicked of the earth
shall drain it down to the dregs.
NRSVCE.9:
But I will rejoice forever;
I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
NRSVCE.10:
All the horns of the wicked I will cut off,
but the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

NET.1: We give thanks to you, O God! We give thanks! You reveal your presence; people tell about your amazing deeds.
NET.2: God says, “At the appointed times, I judge fairly.
NET.3: When the earth and all its inhabitants dissolve in fear, I make its pillars secure.” (Selah)
NET.4: I say to the proud, “Do not be proud,” and to the wicked, “Do not be so confident of victory!
NET.5: Do not be so certain you have won! Do not speak with your head held so high!
NET.6: For victory does not come from the east or west, or from the wilderness.
NET.7: For God is the judge! He brings one down and exalts another.
NET.8: For the LORD holds in his hand a cup full of foaming wine mixed with spices, and pours it out. Surely all the wicked of the earth will slurp it up and drink it to its very last drop.”
NET.9: As for me, I will continually tell what you have done; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob!
NET.10: God says, “I will bring down all the power of the wicked; the godly will be victorious.”
